summary: At the Brookline Select Board meeting on April 14, 2026, discussions focused on roadway safety improvements, particularly for Harvard Street, and the importance of incorporating complete streets policies to enhance safety for cyclists and pedestrians [50:24][64:31][115:28]. The board heard public comments supporting a more pedestrian-friendly design and criticized the prioritization of motor vehicle throughput over safety [60:09][67:12][126:48]. Additionally, the board addressed the need for effective community engagement in the planning process for these projects [123:53][135:05]. No specific votes or decisions were recorded during the meeting, though it emphasized collaboration between the Transportation Board and community stakeholders moving forward [104:54][105:20][236:33].
title: Brookline Select Board Meeting - April 14, 2026
